title: Tarachia pusilla
intro: Tarachia pusilla, also known as the dwarf taraca or small taraca, is a climbing plant species in the family Convolvulaceae. This plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and intestinal parasites. The bioactive constituents of Tarachia pusilla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of a poultice or topical cream to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Tarachia pusilla is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory or bindweed family. This family consists of over 1,800 species of flowering plants, many of which are known for their attractive flowers and climbing or twining habit. The Convolvulaceae family is part of the order Solanales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the potato and eggplant families. The Solanales order is further classified under the larger clade Asterids, which includes many other flowering plant families. Tarachia pusilla is a small, herbaceous plant that grows in tropical and subtropical regions.
